title: Measure and decide the post-JSONL graph mutation architecture
goal: Measure production graph size, mutation rate, rewrite latency and failure exposure, then decide whether to retire JSONL writes in favor of PostgreSQL-only persistence and whether GraphService extraction, append/compaction or cache-structure changes have justified value.

acceptance.assertion: Current and projected node/edge counts, mutation rates, JSONL rewrite durations and recovery frequency are measured from source-owned evidence.
A reversible decision compares PostgreSQL-only writes, append-plus-compaction and continued journaled JSONL operation, including migration and rollback cost.
GraphService or event/change-feed extraction is adopted only if concrete coupling, reuse or throughput evidence justifies the added boundary.
acceptance.verifier: architecture decision with explicit non-adoption path

acceptance.assertion: OrderedCache deletion complexity is benchmarked at relevant scale before replacing the current order vector.

metadata.source: external review follow-ups not required for PR #1440 correctness
metadata.premise: PR #1440 already streams JSONL and has crash recovery; this task must not block the current feature without measured evidence.
